statement.en: Finitary conditions for graph products of monoids
statement.summary: Graph products of monoids provide a common framework for free products and direct products.This paper investigates the interaction of certain finitary conditions with the graph product construction.Specifically, we examine the conditions of being weakly left noetherian (that is, every left ideal is finitely generated) and weakly left coherent (that is, every finitely generated left ideal has a finite presentation) and the related conditions of the ascending chain condition on principal left ideals, being left ideal Howson, and being finitely left equated.All of these conditions, and others, are preserved under retract; as a consequence, if a graph product has such a property, then so do all the constituent monoids.We show that the converse is also true for all the conditions listed except

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper is dated 2026-04-25 (future date, likely preprint/provisional). Core theorem claims appear mathematically sound but require peer validation. The abstract is incomplete (cuts off mid-sentence at 'except'), preventing full evaluation. Mathematical claims about monoid properties are CLASS-A formalizable but the specific preservation results under graph products require complete proof verification.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: グラフの頂点を編むほど、有限条件は逆戻りする——全体と部分が鏡像のように対話する。
